summary.intent: Make clicking a past browser session revive it by loading with the original URL, improving session continuity in the browser viewer.
summary.outcome: Added a 'Revive' button to history cards that creates a new session with the same URL; a separate 'View Log' link handles showing the action log.

repo: Screencast input handlers (scroll/click/keyboard) were previously gated on `screencasting.value`, which could be false when switching to a session with an already-running screencast since `sendStartScreencast` is skipped in that case.
Input handlers now check only `activeSession.value` — any selected session allows input regardless of screencast state.

code.finding: History cards needed a Revive action to re-launch sessions with the original URL; card click was repurposed to show the action log via a 'View Log' link.

workflow: Lint and commit/push were run as sequential bash steps after the edit.

open_items: Revive creates a new session rather than truly restoring state — full session state restoration (beyond URL) may be worth revisiting.
The screencast signal fix (activeSession vs screencasting gate) was shipped just prior; edge cases around session switching and screencast lifecycle may warrant further testing.
